data.executiveSummary: The payment-api service experienced multiple GatewayExceptions due to external API latency spikes, leading to connection pool exhaustion.

rootCause.title: External API latency spikes
rootCause.description: The payment-api service faced multiple GatewayExceptions caused by latency spikes from an external API. This resulted in the exhaustion of the connection pool, preventing new connections from being established.

evidenceChain: Multiple instances of GatewayExceptions logged in payment-api indicating external API latency issues.
Messages consistently reported that the connection pool was exhausted.

timeline.event: GatewayException in payment-api: External API latency spike detected. Connection pool exhausted.
GatewayException in payment-api: External API latency spike detected. Connection pool exhausted.
Multiple GatewayExceptions recorded due to connection pool exhaustion.
Continued GatewayExceptions as the external API latency issues persisted.

data.impact: Users experienced delays or failures in payment processing due to repeated GatewayExceptions, as the payment-api couldn't handle new connections because of exhausted connection pools.

similarIncident.title: Redis Connection Timeout Spikes
similarIncident.resolution: Resolution: Restarted redis-primary-3 instance due to orphaned connections.

recommendedActions: Review connection pool configurations and increase limits to handle spikes in external API latency.
Implement circuit breakers to temporarily halt requests to the external API during high latency periods.
Investigate and address the root causes of the API latency with the third-party provider.
Consider applying the resolution from KAN-6, such as restarting service components in case of orphaned connections.
